sanskrit:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया।
शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा॥
transliteration:
nāhaṃ vedair na tapasā na dānena na cejyayā |
śakya evaṃ-vidho draṣṭuṃ dṛṣṭavān asi māṃ yathā ||
literal_meaning: Not by the Vedas, not by austerity, not by charity, not even by ritual—can this very form be seen as you have seen Me.
contextual_meaning: No amount of knowledge, self-denial, generosity or religious observance can force a direct encounter with the totality of Being; grace is given only when the ego stops bargaining and surrenders in love.
psychological_state: Cognitive accommodation failure—previous schemas (scholar, ascetic, donor, ritualist) collapse, forcing a shift from merit-based identity to relational receptivity.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving client who has tried every self-help technique, meditation app and charity marathon still feels empty; in therapy they realize the goal was never another achievement but allowing themselves to be ‘seen’ in vulnerable presence.
anchor_text: Grace reveals the whole when the ego surrenders its bargains.
